redGreenRefactorEvidence.failingTestEvidence: The workspace contract failed three assertions because packageManager, apps/demo, shared packages, and Demo sources did not exist.
redGreenRefactorEvidence.passingTestEvidence: The same contract passed all four tests after adding the pinned pnpm/Turbo workspace and documented package surfaces.
redGreenRefactorEvidence.refactorEvidence: Kept import rules structural and public-surface based so they reject app/package boundary violations without coupling to implementation details.

redGreenRefactorEvidence.failingTestEvidence: The first component run failed before assertions because Vitest globals and cleanup were not configured; the corrected run then exposed the icon-hidden accessible name contract.
redGreenRefactorEvidence.passingTestEvidence: Home and shell tests passed with exactly two launcher links, four discoverable destinations, no forbidden Home content, and no fetch calls.
redGreenRefactorEvidence.refactorEvidence: Centralized deterministic fixtures, shared accessible React primitives, and test cleanup while preserving icons as aria-hidden decoration.

commands.outputSummary: 2 behavior assertions failed: cleanup isolation and hidden-icon accessible name

commands.command: corepack pnpm --filter @juli/demo test && corepack pnpm --filter @juli/utils test

redGreenRefactorEvidence.failingTestEvidence: The independent quality gate failed lint because the app wordmark used a native internal anchor and PostCSS exported an anonymous object.
redGreenRefactorEvidence.passingTestEvidence: After the minimal framework-correct refactor, lint, type-check, tests, package builds, and the static Next.js production build all passed.
redGreenRefactorEvidence.refactorEvidence: Used next/link at the app boundary, kept shared UI framework-independent, and verified desktop/mobile grids, 44px targets, a 3px focus-visible ring, and reduced-motion styles in the production runtime.

commands.outputSummary: Lint rejected internal native anchor and anonymous default export
lint, type-check, 4 Demo tests, shared package builds, and Next production build passed

redGreenRefactorEvidence.failingTestEvidence: The pre-Phase 2.6 workspace transition contract still required pnpm, Turbo, apps/demo, and packages/* to be absent.
redGreenRefactorEvidence.passingTestEvidence: The superseded contract now preserves dashboard npm independence while requiring the Phase 2.6 root workspace; strengthened boundary tests also scan all apps, package manifests, and package dependency cycles.
redGreenRefactorEvidence.refactorEvidence: Removed the stale root npm lock while retaining apps/dashboard/package-lock.json, making root pnpm ownership and dashboard npm ownership explicit.

commands.outputSummary: 5 failed, 1 passed on superseded removal assertions

implementationSummary: Added a pinned pnpm 10/Turborepo workspace and independently buildable Next.js 16 Demo app. The responsive shell exposes exactly four Vietnamese destinations; Home renders exactly two accessible shared-package launchers over deterministic no-network fixtures. Semantic tokens, React primitives, and Vietnamese formatters now originate in documented shared packages, with import-boundary tests and a dedicated PR CI job.
assumptions: Issue #397 owns shell placeholders for Decisions, Analytics, and Settings but not their feature content.
Native anchors are acceptable inside framework-independent @juli/ui primitives; apps may provide framework-specific adapters in later slices.
The parent/child workflow cache remains local because the repository intentionally ignores workflow-cache artifacts and slice-routing configuration.
risks: The root pnpm lock includes the existing npm-managed dashboard workspace; dashboard CI remains on npm until a dedicated migration removes the dual-lock transition.
Public DNS, TLS, and standalone server process wiring remain outside this Home foundation issue.
The local dev watcher hit the machine open-file limit, so runtime verification used the successful production build/server instead.
